tasks.description: Make DynamicEffectRegistry reactive: add _version counter, _listeners set, subscribe/getSnapshot arrow methods, _notify() private method, and batch() for suppressing notifications during bulk registration. Call _notify() at end of register(), registerAsync(), unregister() only when state actually changed. Extend dynamic record type to include optional schema?: ParameterSchema. Add getSchema(name) method. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts

Wire reactivity into useEffectRegistry: (1) Add useSyncExternalStore(registry.subscribe, registry.getSnapshot) after the useMemo so InnerProvider re-renders when async registration completes. (2) For the first-render timing fix: use useLayoutEffect instead of useEffect for replaceEffectRegistry(registry) — this runs synchronously after render but before children paint, closing the first-render gap while keeping render pure. (3) Change the Promise.all registration to sequential-by-priority using registry.batch(): drafts first (lowest), then DB effects (with console.warn if db.length > 0 about legacy deprecation), then resource effects (highest, passing effect.parameterSchema). batch() suppresses notifications until all registrations complete, then fires one _notify(). File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts

Create EffectErrorBoundary class component. Props: { effectName: string; fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode }. On error: getDerivedStateFromError sets hasError: true. componentDidCatch logs '[EffectErrorBoundary] "{effectName}" runtime error: {error.message}'. render() returns this.props.fallback when errored, this.props.children otherwise. Reset when effectName changes (compare in getDerivedStateFromError or use key= at call sites). Keep under 50 lines. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/EffectErrorBoundary.tsx

Create validateAndCoerceParams utility. Takes (params: Record<string,unknown> | undefined, schema: ParameterSchema | undefined) => Record<string,unknown>. Fast path: return params ?? {} if schema is undefined/empty. Otherwise: start with defaults built inline (replicating getFallbackValue logic from ParameterControls — do NOT import from the component to avoid layering inversion). Overlay user params with per-type coercion: number → typeof check + clamp(min,max), boolean → typeof check, select → validate against options, color → regex /^#[0-9a-f]{3,8}$/i. Wrong types fall back to default. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts. Keep under 60 lines.

Wire EffectErrorBoundary and validateAndCoerceParams into wrapWithClipEffects (effects/index.tsx). For each effect category (continuous, entrance, exit): (1) capture pre-wrap content as beforeX variable, (2) call validateAndCoerceParams(clip.X.params, getEffectRegistry().getSchema(clip.X.type)) for the params prop, (3) wrap the effect JSX in <EffectErrorBoundary effectName={clip.X.type} fallback={beforeX}>. Preserve exact existing prop contracts: continuous gets effectFrames omitted (not passed currently), entrance/exit get effectFrames=secondsToFrames(clip.X.duration, fps). Extract registry once at top: const registry = getEffectRegistry().

Wire EffectErrorBoundary and validateAndCoerceParams into EffectLayerSequence (compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx). Wrap the <Effect> component with <EffectErrorBoundary effectName={clip.continuous.type} fallback={<>{children}</>}>. Pass validated params: params={validateAndCoerceParams(clip.continuous.params, getEffectRegistry().getSchema(clip.continuous.type))}. Preserve existing contract: effectFrames={durationInFrames} for continuous effects.

Align EffectCreatorPanel preview with timeline rendering (components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx). (1) Create local wrapPreviewEffect function that mirrors wrapWithClipEffects prop shapes: for continuous category, pass effectFrames={durationInFrames} (matching EffectLayerSequence); for entrance/exit, pass effectFrames from previewParams.effectSeconds * fps. (2) Wrap the effect in <EffectErrorBoundary effectName='preview' fallback={<PreviewRect assetSrc={assetSrc}/>}>. (3) Keep PREVIEW_FPS=30 and PREVIEW_SIZE=320 — the plan's FPS-from-context is not implementable without threading fps as a prop (accepted tradeoff per FLAG-004). Replace makePreviewComposition to use wrapPreviewEffect.

Add @deprecated JSDoc to useEffects.ts:6. Add: /** @deprecated Legacy effects table — use resource-based effects via useEffectResources instead. */ above the useEffects function. File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffects.ts

Extend tests in DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx: (1) Test subscribe/getSnapshot: register an effect, assert getSnapshot incremented and listener was called. (2) Test dedup skip: register same code twice, assert listener fires only once. (3) Test batch: register multiple effects inside batch(), assert listener fires exactly once at end. (4) Test getSchema: register with schema, assert getSchema returns it. (5) Test EffectErrorBoundary: render a component that throws inside the boundary with a fallback div, assert fallback renders. (6) Test validateAndCoerceParams: feed wrong-type params and assert coercion (number string→default, invalid select→default, bad color→default). Existing 2 tests must still pass.

Run all video-editor tests and fix any failures. Run: npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/ --reporter=verbose. Must pass: DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx, useEffectResources.test.tsx (if exists), VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx. Also run TypeScript check: npx tsc --noEmit to catch type errors in changed files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until green.

Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.

watch_items: RENDER PURITY (correctness-1/5): Plan says replaceEffectRegistry() in render. Use useLayoutEffect instead — runs before paint but after render, keeping render pure. This is the safer alternative endorsed by the gate.

BATCH NOTIFICATIONS (performance-1, all_locations): DynamicEffectRegistry.batch(fn) must suppress _notify() calls during fn execution and fire one _notify() at the end. Without this, sequential registration causes O(n) InnerProvider re-renders.

PREVIEW FPS (FLAG-004, correctness-2, callers): EffectCreatorPanel has no fps prop and no context exposes it. Keep PREVIEW_FPS=30 hardcoded — this is an accepted tradeoff. Do NOT try to thread fps from context.

LAYERING INVERSION (conventions flag): validateParams.ts lives in effects/ — do NOT import getDefaultValues from components/ParameterControls.tsx. Duplicate the getFallbackValue logic inline in the utility to maintain layer separation.

EFFECT LAYER CONTRACT: EffectLayerSequence passes effectFrames={durationInFrames} for continuous effects. wrapWithClipEffects does NOT pass effectFrames for continuous (only for entrance/exit). Preserve both contracts exactly.
CONTINUOUS EFFECT in wrapWithClipEffects: Current code (line 129-136) does NOT pass effectFrames to continuous effects — only durationInFrames and intensity. Do not add effectFrames here.
BUILT-IN PRIORITY INVARIANT: DynamicEffectRegistry.get() returns builtIn[name] ?? dynamic[name]. This must remain — built-ins always win over dynamic effects with same name.
DEBT ITEMS — do not make worse: [DEBT] video-editor-effect-registry, [DEBT] video-editor-effect-preview, [DEBT] clip-param-schema-evolution
CustomEffectEditor.tsx is dead code (no callers) — do NOT modify or migrate it. Out of scope per SD-005.

sense_checks.question: Does DynamicEffectRegistry expose subscribe() and getSnapshot() as arrow-function properties (not prototype methods) so they have stable references for useSyncExternalStore? Does batch() correctly suppress notifications and fire exactly one at end?

Is replaceEffectRegistry() called in useLayoutEffect (not useEffect or render body)? Does useSyncExternalStore subscribe to registry? Are effects registered in priority order (drafts→DB→resources) inside a batch() call?
Does EffectErrorBoundary render the fallback prop (not children) when an error occurs? Is it a class component with getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch? Does it log the effectName?

Does validateAndCoerceParams live in effects/ (not components/)? Does it implement its own fallback logic without importing from ParameterControls? Does it handle all 4 types: number (clamp), boolean, select (option validation), color (regex)?

In wrapWithClipEffects: does continuous NOT pass effectFrames? Do entrance/exit preserve secondsToFrames(clip.X.duration, fps)? Does each boundary get the correct effectName (clip.continuous.type, clip.entrance.type, clip.exit.type)?
Does EffectLayerSequence pass effectFrames={durationInFrames} (its existing contract)? Is the fallback <>{children}</> so clips render without the effect on error?

Does wrapPreviewEffect mirror the prop shapes from wrapWithClipEffects? For continuous: effectFrames=durationInFrames. For entrance/exit: effectFrames from effect seconds. Is PREVIEW_FPS still 30 (not from context)?
Does useEffects.ts have @deprecated JSDoc above the function?

Do the new tests cover: reactivity (subscribe fires), dedup (subscribe doesn't fire), batch (one notification), getSchema, EffectErrorBoundary fallback rendering, and validateAndCoerceParams coercion? Do existing 2 tests still pass?
Do all video-editor tests pass? Does tsc --noEmit pass? Were any regressions introduced in VisualClip.tsx or TextClip.tsx callers of wrapWithClipEffects (signature unchanged)?
Did the verification tests pass? Were any regressions found and fixed?

meta_commentary:
Key execution decisions the plan leaves to the executor:

1. **useLayoutEffect over render-time mutation**: The plan says replaceEffectRegistry() in render (Step 1.2). The gate explicitly says useLayoutEffect is an acceptable safer alternative. USE useLayoutEffect — it runs synchronously before paint, closing the first-render gap without violating React purity. This satisfies the relaxed criterion: "module singleton available before first child render."

2. **batch() API design**: Add a simple `batch(fn: () => Promise<void>)` method to DynamicEffectRegistry that sets a `_batching = true` flag, awaits fn, then sets `_batching = false` and calls `_notify()` once. The `_notify()` method should early-return when `_batching` is true. This prevents O(n) re-renders during initialization.

3. **validateParams layering**: The plan says reuse getDefaultValues from ParameterControls. The conventions flag correctly identifies this as a layering inversion (effects/ importing from components/). Instead, duplicate the ~15 lines of getFallbackValue logic directly in validateParams.ts. This is deliberate duplication to maintain layer boundaries — it's the right call.

4. **Continuous effect contract divergence**: wrapWithClipEffects (index.tsx:128-136) does NOT pass effectFrames to continuous effects — only durationInFrames and intensity. EffectLayerSequence (line 42) passes effectFrames={durationInFrames}. Both are correct for their contexts. The preview's wrapPreviewEffect should use the EffectLayerSequence pattern for continuous (effectFrames=durationInFrames) since it's a standalone preview.

5. **Preview FPS**: Keep hardcoded at 30. Do NOT try to thread fps from context — accepted tradeoff, would require plumbing changes to ClipPanel and DataProviderContext that are out of scope.

6. **TypeScript**: registerAsync signature gains optional `schema?: ParameterSchema`. This is backward-compatible — all existing callers pass 2 args. Only resource registration passes the third arg.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Make DynamicEffectRegistry reactive with subscribe/getSnapshot + fix first-render timing
Step 2: Create EffectErrorBoundary with explicit fallback prop
Step 3: Wire EffectErrorBoundary into wrapWithClipEffects, EffectLayerSequence, EffectCreatorPanel
Step 4: Extend tests for reactivity, dedup, batch, error boundary, param validation
Step 5: Store parameterSchema in DynamicEffectRegistry alongside effects
Step 6: Create validateAndCoerceParams utility with type coercion
Step 7: Apply validateAndCoerceParams at render-time in wrapWithClipEffects and EffectLayerSequence
Step 8: Consolidate effect sources with explicit priority + deprecate useEffects.ts
Step 9: Align EffectCreatorPanel preview with timeline wrapping

validation.completeness_notes: All 11 plan steps mapped to tasks. Steps 5 and 1 are combined into T1 since both modify DynamicEffectRegistry.ts. Steps 7 and 3 share T5/T6 since error boundary wiring and param validation apply to the same call sites. Step 11 (manual smoke tests) is info-only per the plan and is noted in T10's scope.
